page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-60 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 60 vCPUs mapped to 30 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is provisioned with 118.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of approximately 1.97 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU evaluations. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI, which contribute to strong performance in encryption and physics simulations. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth ranks in the top 25%, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the middle 50%.

This configuration is suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations, all of which demonstrated strong benchmark results. It also supports moderate LLM inference tasks, showing strong text generation speeds on small and medium models, though prompt processing for larger models remains average. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio of under 2 GB per core represents a tradeoff, making this server less suitable for memory-intensive datasets despite its high compute density.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-60 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server configured with 60 vCPUs and 118.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 30 physical cores with multithreading and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. The hardware delivers strong multi-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. It supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, facilitating strong performance in encryption, ray tracing, and software compilation. However, its memory-to-vCPU ratio is low at 1.97 GB per core, and large-block memory bandwidth remains average. This server is suited for compute-bound workloads, database operations, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference, offering high compute density at the expense of memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-60 features 60 vCPUs and 118 GB RAM on AMD EPYC 9B14. It delivers strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-60 is a compute-optimized server with 60 vCPUs, 118 GB RAM, and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A compute-dense x86_64 server featuring 60 vCPUs and AVX-512 support, optimized for multi-threaded processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 60 vCPUs and 30 physical cores.
Equipped with 118.0 GB of RAM, providing 1.97 GB of memory per core.
Strong multi-core performance in Geekbench, Passmark, and stress-ng benchmarks.
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets for accelerated processing.
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and text processing.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, focusing purely on CPU compute.
